# solutions_connectors_tables_mapping.csv

**Generated by:** [`map_solutions_connectors_tables.py`](../map_solutions_connectors_tables.md)
**Granularity:** One row per (solution, connector, table) tuple, fully denormalized
**Type:** Backward-compatibility / legacy denormalized view

## Overview

Legacy wide table that joins solution metadata, connector metadata, and table references into a single denormalized CSV. Maintained for backward compatibility with older scripts and workflows.

> **For new integrations, prefer the normalized files** — [`connectors.csv`](connectors.md), [`solutions.csv`](solutions.md), [`tables.csv`](tables.md), [`solutions_connectors_tables_mapping_simplified.csv`](solutions_connectors_tables_mapping_simplified.md) — which are smaller and easier to maintain.

## Use Cases

- **Legacy integrations** — scripts written before the normalized split that expect this single file.
- **Single-table imports** — quick exploration in tools that don't support joins (Excel pivot tables).
- **Kusto upload** — uploaded as `solution_analyzer_full_mapping` by `upload_to_kusto.py`.

## Columns

| Column | Description | Data Source |
|--------|-------------|-------------|
| `Table` | Table name (empty for solutions without data connectors) | Connector table detection |
| `solution_name` | Solution folder name | Parent solution folder |
| `solution_folder` | Solution folder name | File system |
| `solution_github_url` | Full GitHub URL to the solution's folder | Computed |
| `solution_publisher_id` | Publisher ID | SolutionMetadata.json |
| `solution_offer_id` | Offer ID | SolutionMetadata.json |
| `solution_first_publish_date` | First publication date | SolutionMetadata.json |
| `solution_last_publish_date` | Last update date | SolutionMetadata.json |
| `solution_version` | Solution version | Solution JSON or SolutionMetadata.json |
| `solution_support_name` | Support provider name | SolutionMetadata.json |
| `solution_support_tier` | Support tier (Microsoft, Partner, Community) | SolutionMetadata.json |
| `solution_support_link` | Support link URL | SolutionMetadata.json |
| `solution_author_name` | Author name | Solution JSON |
| `solution_categories` | Comma-separated categories | SolutionMetadata.json |
| `connector_id` | Connector identifier (empty for solutions without data connectors) | Connector JSON |
| `connector_publisher` | Connector publisher | Connector JSON |
| `connector_title` | Connector display title | Connector JSON |
| `connector_description` | Connector description (newlines replaced with `<br>` for GitHub CSV rendering) | Connector JSON |
| `connector_instruction_steps` | Setup and configuration instructions (JSON-encoded) | Connector JSON |
| `connector_permissions` | Required permissions (JSON-encoded) | Connector JSON |
| `connector_files` | Semicolon-separated GitHub URLs to connector definition files | File system |
| `is_unique` | `true` if table appears in only one connector file | Computed |
| `table_detection_methods` | (Optional, only with `--show-detection-methods`) Semicolon-separated list of detection methods | Analysis tracking |

> Newlines in `connector_description` and `connector_permissions` are replaced with `<br>` tags for proper rendering in GitHub's CSV viewer. `connector_instruction_steps` uses standard JSON encoding with `\n`.

## Related CSVs

- [`solutions_connectors_tables_mapping_simplified.csv`](solutions_connectors_tables_mapping_simplified.md) — same edges as 3 columns instead of denormalized.
- [`connectors.csv`](connectors.md), [`solutions.csv`](solutions.md), [`tables.csv`](tables.md) — preferred normalized entity files.
